title: inconsistent behavior for nested containers and podman run
body:
### Issue Description

I have an application that runs multiple podman containers in a nested rootless container and unfortunately I've been seeing sporadic errors that cause the application to fail consistently once the condition has been triggered.

I believe this is due to some state stored in /var/lib/containers that I have stored in a podman volume. but its onclear. I also see similar issues when containers are started up concurrently.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

1. clone github.com/egdaemon/eg
2. go install ./cmd/...
3. ~/go/bin/eg compute local



### Describe the results you received

Sometimes when a podman exec is executed and fails (think of something like a test case failing) the container in all future runs returns:

```
Error: OCI runtime error: crun: controller `pids` is not available under /sys/fs/cgroup/machine.slice/libpod-313cbda1c0020d92cd9f1f6172eab34ea52058d7264fac06b0c7fe88df8f7c43.scope/container/machine.slice/libpod-6074648255e4b1d4a18a6dfe358f9e695f5183af38b710f8370380c802b239e9.scope/container/cgroup.controllers
```



### Describe the results you expected

expected the container to run without issue on subsequent attempts.

### Podman in a container

Yes

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

_No response_

### Additional information

I'm able to work around the issue by rebuilding the container at the final step in the containerfile using a cache busting command:
```
RUN echo "cache buster 474ba506-2797-505a-ae80-b41535886d78"
```

but this shouldnt be necessary.

comments.body:
Hi @james-lawrence, your project looks really cool but I don't think it's reasonable for the reproducer steps to include it. 

Are you able to reproduce this just using Podman commands? For example a script that creates the host Podman container how your project does, then a loop that churns creating/exec'ing containers inside the host Podman container until the error is triggered.

It's not clear to me at what layer your error message is coming from but two things to check would be the `podman inspect` output from a working and non working container and the Podman info output from both the host Podman and nested Podman to compare.

Another thing, if it's the nested container throwing the error, depending on your use case it may be an option for you to just disable cgroups when launching the inner containers with `podman run --cgroups=disabled`, maybe even if just used as a troubleshooting step to see if the issue is still reproducible.

A reviewer has determined we need more information to understand the reported issue.  A comment on what is missing should be provided. Be certain you:

* provide an exact reproducer where possible
* verify you have provided all relevant information - minimum is `podman info`
* answer any follow up questions

If no response to the `needs-info` is provided in 30 days, this issue may be closed by our stale bot.

For more information on reporting issues on this repository, consult our [issue guide](ISSUE.md).

@ninja-quokka thanks. included it because thats probably the simplest way to repro atm since i havent had the bandwidth to isolate it, and given how its presenting manually isolating it would be a giant pain in the ass (nested containers + concurrency). unfortunately it started cropping up more recently without any changes on my part beyond a distro update for the containers. (new environment/new version of podman)

I did try --cgroupsns=host previously which didnt help. but messing with cgroups for something that is presenting like a race condition/data corruption feels pretty kludgey.

I agree with you that setting --cgroups=disabled is more of a workaround/debugging step than a resolution to the issue. I have gone and done it and the issue has disappeared. but that isnt terribly surprising, entirely disabling the system that causes the problem would mask the issue. =)

I'm happy to slow roll this issue and do information gathering for whomever has knowledge on the conmon/cgroups codepaths because this definitely feels like a real issue and not just a mess up on my end and I'd like to help podman's devs dig into it.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
